nysed-ela-2025-g4-mc-q1.alt: Question 1. What does the word “muffles” mean in paragraph 3? Choices: A: changes to a slow speed B: moves to a new stage C: causes things to get darker D: makes things difficult to notice

nysed-ela-2025-g4-mc-q2.alt: Question 2. What is a central idea of the section “All Through the Night...”? Choices: A: Falling asleep takes less than thirty minutes for most people. B: Odd feelings while falling asleep are normal. C: Time passes differently while falling asleep. D: Falling asleep is an experience that involves the whole body.

nysed-ela-2025-g4-mc-q3.alt: Question 3. Which detail best expresses a claim the author makes about sleep? Choices: A: “Sleep is so useful that all animals do it. And without it, waking life would be impossible.” (paragraph 2) B: “As you slip into sleep, your heart slows down and you breathe more slowly.” (paragraph 4) C: “During deep sleep, long, slow waves of electrical impulses move through the brain.” (paragraph 7) D: “In the next stage of sleep, your muscles go completely slack. Your body doesn’t move at all.” (paragraph 8)

nysed-ela-2025-g4-mc-q4.alt: Question 4. Based on the article, how does REM sleep help people? Choices: A: The eyes move back and forth while they are closed. B: The brain links experiences and memories. C: People can begin having strange dreams. D: People sleep more deeply when it is almost morning.

nysed-ela-2025-g4-mc-q5.alt: Question 5. How does the author organize the information in paragraph 14? Choices: A: by stating a problem with sleep and then presenting a solution B: by sharing a cause-and-effect situation when sleep is interrupted C: by explaining the steps to learning a new game D: by comparing how long people dream at night

nysed-ela-2025-g4-mc-q6.alt: Question 6. Which sentence best describes how the chart adds to the information in the article? Choices: A: It explains how sleep helps keep the body healthy. B: It shares the steps to fall asleep easily. C: It offers facts about how the body reacts to sleep. D: It compares being awake to being asleep.

nysed-ela-2025-g4-mc-q19.alt: Question 19. How does the author organize the information in paragraphs 5 through 10? Choices: A: by comparing each item that Dustin pulls out of the wallet B: by giving the reasons why Dustin must search for the wallet’s owner C: by listing the order of events that lead to Dustin finding the wallet D: by showing how the clues cause Dustin to find the wallet’s owner

nysed-ela-2025-g4-mc-q20.alt: Question 20. What do the details in paragraph 13 reveal about Salvador? Choices: A: His belongings often get lost on the train. B: He enjoys visiting the city where Dustin lives. C: He is grateful for Dustin’s act of kindness. D: His family is very important to him.

nysed-ela-2025-g4-mc-q21.alt: Question 21. Which statement best explains why Dustin claims, “This has been the best adventure ever!” in paragraph 17? Choices: A: Dustin’s actions lead to an invitation to Rocket Land with Salvador’s family. B: Marisa is proud and makes Dustin’s favorite cake to celebrate. C: Dustin has made the day pass quickly by searching for the owner of the wallet. D: Salvador’s wallet has many clues to help Dustin solve the mystery.

nysed-ela-2025-g4-mc-q22.alt: Question 22. What does the word “approval” suggest as used in paragraph 25? Choices: A: Everyone is ready to leave for Rocket Land. B: Dustin is allowed to sit with Hugo and Carlos. C: The boys may eat the snacks Emerita has baked. D: The blue van will take everyone to Rocket Land.

nysed-ela-2025-g4-mc-q23.alt: Question 23. Which sentence best states a theme of the story? Choices: A: The best mysteries are solved using teamwork. B: People in different cities can have a lot in common. C: Helping others can bring surprising rewards. D: Pets are important for bringing families together.

nysed-ela-2025-g4-mc-q26.alt: Question 26. How is paragraph 3 organized in the story? Choices: A: by making comparisons between two characters B: by telling the solution to a problem C: by giving the answer to a question D: by describing events in the order they happen

nysed-ela-2025-g4-mc-q27.alt: Question 27. Read this sentence from paragraph 6. I rattled her courtyard fence and called, “Are you there?” What does the word “rattled” mean as used in this sentence? Choices: A: made someone feel excited B: noticed a door or a gate C: walked around to wait for someone D: made a noise by moving something

nysed-ela-2025-g4-mc-q28.alt: Question 28. What do Miss Sabines’ actions in paragraph 8 reveal most about her? Choices: A: She cares about the narrator. B: She thinks the narrator is funny. C: She is a very curious person. D: She likes when people visit her.

nysed-ela-2025-g4-mc-q29.alt: Question 29. Which sentence from the story best describes the setting? Choices: A: “Even so, I enjoyed making the steep but quick (ten-minute) climb to Miss Sabines’ house.” (paragraph 2) B: “It has a big garden, with beautiful desert plants and herbs growing everywhere, and a courtyard that has tiles painted bright colors, with sculptures, pinwheels, wind chimes, and lawn ornaments scattered throughout.” (paragraph 5) C: “The pleasant voice came floating across the courtyard walls, putting me instantly at ease.” (paragraph 7) D: “Miss Sabines showed me some of her new plantings, we played with her pets, and I leafed through a collection of photographs by Ansel Adams, whom Miss Sabines calls a ‘poet of Southwestern landscapes.’” (paragraph 11)

nysed-ela-2025-g4-mc-q30.alt: Question 30. Sometimes a place can be special to a person. Which detail best shows how this idea is developed in the story? Choices: A: “The sun was turning the Sangre de Cristo Mountains a rosy gold color.” (paragraph 1) B: “Speaking of daydreams, I often fantasize about living in a house like Miss Sabines’.” (paragraph 5) C: “We passed the rest of the afternoon puttering around the house.” (paragraph 11) D: “‘We’re having a party tonight, and I’ve been gone for hours!’” (paragraph 12)

nysed-ela-2025-g4-mc-q31.alt: Question 31. Which sentence best states a theme of the story? Choices: A: It is important for people to not waste their time. B: Hard work always pays off in the end. C: Friends can help us feel better when we are upset. D: It is important to respect your elders.
